title: Elaphoglossum spatulatum
intro: Elaphoglossum spatulatum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as a type of epiphytic or terrestrial fern native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and oxidative stress, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Elaphoglossum spatulatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Elaphoglossum spatulatum is often used in teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as in combination with other medicinal plants to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Elaphoglossum spatulatum is classified within the family Osmundaceae. However, some sources consider it to belong to the family Osmundaceae or the family Thelypteridaceae depending on the source. The genus Elaphoglossum is a part of the family Osmundaceae. It is also classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and their allies. The species Elaphoglossum spatulatum is further classified under the order Osmundales.
uses: Elaphoglossum spatulatum is a plant species that has been found to have potential medicinal properties. However, there is limited information available on its specific uses.
